content.full_text:
STEVE and JILL HARVEY
3320 Morningside Drive, Richmond, CA 94803 • (415) 222-[illegible]

May 18, 1990

Mr. John Rothman
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Rothman:

     Would you please confirm, in writing, your statement to me
by telephone on May 9, 1990 that I have no conflict as a Richmond
planning commissioner in voting on a project that involves my
wife's stepfather?

     As you may recall, my wife's stepfather and I have no
financial connections of any kind. If you could please expedite
your reply, as you mentioned, I would be most grateful.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Steve Harvey

San Juan Capistrano
Community
Redevelopment
Agency

FPPC

Jul 9 8 51 AM '90

[SEAL/LOGO: City of San Juan Capistrano]

June 29, 1990

Ms. Susan Bobrow
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street - Suite 100
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Letter No. 90-377/Request for Additional Information

Dear Ms. Bobrow:

This letter is provided to you at the request of City Attorney John R. Shaw. Mr. Shaw relates that you need additional information which explains the scope of legal services under a consulting agreement between the Community Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Juan Capistrano and Consultant Nancy Erickson.

Section 1 of Mrs. Erickson's agreement provides that all work performed by her shall be subject to review and approval by myself, acting as Community Redevelopment Agency Executive Director. The full time employee staffing of the Redevelopment Agency is as follows: I am the Executive Director of the Agency, Jeff Parker is my assistant and Cassandra Walker is the Community Redevelopment Agency Manager. Since Mrs. Erickson became a consultant to the Agency, she reports directly to Mr. Parker and Ms. Walker. Her services are reviewed by these two individuals. They in turn provide an update regarding the status of Agency projects to me. I give specific direction regarding communications and negotiations carried on between the Agency and project redevelopers. All Redevelopment Agency Redevelopment agreements are ultimately approved or rejected by the Redevelopment Agency Board.

You have asked for additional information regarding items 3-A and 3-F under Exhibit A to the Consulting Agreement. The use of the word "negotiate" under scope of services means that Mrs. Erickson is authorized to take specific direction from Redevelopment Agency employees, including myself, and convey that direction to a given project redeveloper. Information from such meetings is then relayed to my Agency staff for independent review which is then reviewed by myself.

32400 Paseo Adelanto
San Juan Capistrano
California 92675
714-493-1171

June 29, 1990
Page 2

To take a case in point, the Redevelopment Agency consummated a lease
arrangement with a restaurateur who will be opening a restaurant within real
property owned by the Redevelopment Agency. (This particular project has nothing
to do with the Franciscan Plaza II which is discussed Mr. Shaw's letter) Mrs.
Erickson was involved in several discussions with the restaurateur. That process
involved direction being given by my subordinates and myself to Mrs. Erickson which
was then relayed via a vis Mrs. Erickson to the restaurateur. Mrs. Erickson had no
authority to finalize deal points. To the extent that she had any advice or
recommendation on developing the lease agreement, her views were substantively
reviewed by my Agency subordinate as well as myself. The lease agreement was
eventually reviewed and approved per the final decision-making body, the
Redevelopment Agency.

With respect to Exhibit A, Item 3F, Mrs. Erickson has not attended any City Council
meetings, Planning Commission meetings, Redevelopment Agency meetings, or
Architectural Review Board meetings to date. Section 3-F was intended to deal
mainly with Mrs. Erickson's possible attendance at Redevelopment Agency meetings
where she would be available to answer factual questions.

Please be also advised that this is to confirm that Mrs. Erickson did not perform any
consulting services with respect to Phase II of the Franciscan Plaza project as noted
in Mr. Shaw's letter of May 22, 1990.

I have personal knowledge of the above stated facts and am competent to testify as
to the truth of these facts if called as a witness.

If you should need any additional information, please feel free to call this office.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Stephen B. Julian
Executive Director

SBJ:ms
cc: J. Shaw

DRUG USE
IS LIFE ABUSE

FPP3
MEMBERS OF THE CITY COUNCIL
ANTHONY L. BLAND
LAWRENCE F. BUCHHEIM
KENNETH H. GOAD
GARY L. HAUSDORFER
PHILLIP R. SCHWARTZE

CITY MANAGER
STEPHEN B JULIAN

May 22, 1990


Ms. Katherine Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street - Sixth Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Informal Opinion

Dear Ms. Donovan:

On behalf of San Juan Capistrano Planning Commissioner, James Erickson, this letter
is to request an informal written opinion pursuant to Title 2, California Administrative Code Section 18329(c)(2). The specific questions are whether Planning
Commissioner Erickson would:

a) Have a conflict of interest under Government Code Section
87100 had he voted on a project known as Franciscan Plaza,
Phase II; and

b) Necessarily be disqualified from voting on land use applications
before the Planning Commission involving proposed projects
located within the redevelopment project area where:

i) His wife, Nancy Erickson, has performed consulting
work of the nature described below for the
Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Juan
Capistrano relating to projects which are later
reviewed by the City Planning Commission for
planning and zoning and other related land use
entitlements;

ii) His wife has not performed any consulting work
relating to a project in the redevelopment area
which later is reviewed by the Planning Commission
for planning and zoning entitlements.


32400 PASEO ADELANTO, SAN JUAN CAPISTRANO, CALIFORNIA 92675 • (714) 493-1171

Ms. Katherine Donovan
May 22, 1990
Page Two

FACTS

1. Planning Commissioner James Erickson was appointed to the San Juan Capistrano Planning Commission on November 21, 1989.

2. In April 1990, an Architectural Approval Application and Zone Variance Request for a commercial retail project came before the Planning Commission for approval. This project is known as Franciscan Plaza, Phase II. The project is located within the redevelopment project area formed by the "Community Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Juan Capistrano". The Redevelopment Agency is a separate governmental entity existing under Health and Safety Code Sections 33000, et seq.

3. Planning Commissioner Erickson did not vote on this project in that he was out of town on business. He did, however, wish to vote had he been able to attend this Planning Commission meeting. The Planning Commission did vote on the matter and the land use approvals are now pending before the City Council.

4. Planning Commissioner Erickson's wife, Mrs. Nancy Erickson, was a full time employee of the City of San Juan Capistrano through July 31, 1989. Her job position was Community Development Administrator serving the Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Juan Capistrano. Her job duties included, but were not limited to, day to day management and administration responsibilities for redevelopment projects within the redevelopment area. During her employment tenure, prior to July 31, 1989, Mrs. Erickson assisted the Redevelopment Agency in consummating a development and disposition agreement between the Redevelopment Agency and the developer of Franciscan Plaza, Phase I. The development and disposition agreement provided for the construction of a parking facility on the project site, that is the Phase I portion of the project. The Agency, under the agreement, would assist in financing a portion of the structure in return for which the Agency would receive rights to use a portion of the parking facility. Mrs. Erickson did not perform any work as an employee with respect to any aspect of Phase II of the Franciscan Plaza project.

5. After leaving employment with the City, Mrs. Erickson entered into an independent contractor agreement with the Redevelopment Agency on August 1, 1989. (see enclosure) Under the agreement, she is to provide advice and information to the Redevelopment Agency. She has no decision making authority. Neither she, nor Commissioner Erickson, have any say in determining the extent of work to be performed under the independent contractor agreement. Further, this independent contractor agreement is not in any way reviewed by the City Planning Commission. Her role under the agreement is to provide analysis, advice, etc. to Redevelopment Agency staff on an "on call" basis.

Ms. Katherine Donovan
May 22, 1990
Page Three

6. Since entering into the independent contractor agreement, Mrs. Erickson
has submitted billings for work performed relating to certain portions of
Phase I of the Franciscan Plaza. The Franciscan Plaza project consists to
date of two phases with respect to required planning entitlements. Phase I
consisted of an architectural approval and site plan layout of retail space,
a theatre, and the parking structure. Phase I was approved by the Planning
Commission prior to Mr. Erickson's appointment to the Planning
commission. Mrs. Erickson's billings are based on work performed in
providing background information to new Redevelopment Agency
employees to "bring them up to speed" with respect to the development
and disposition agreement between Agency and the developer and these
particular billings are limited solely to the Phase I portion of the project.

The above billings occurred between September 1989 to the present time.
The total billings submitted by Mrs. Erickson to the Agency involving her
Phase I consultation and a host of other tasks performed unrelated to the
Franciscan Plaza project now amount to more than $1,000, but less than
$20,000.

7. The architectural application and zone variance application before the
Planning Commission are land use entitlement applications separate and
distinct from the Redevelopment Agency agreement. The Planning Commission does not review or act upon any Redevelopment Agency
agreements. Conversely, the Redevelopment Agency does not in any way
act upon the planning and zoning entitlements discussed herein.

8. Funds paid by the Agency to Mrs. Erickson are solely Agency funds. The
Franciscan Plaza developer has not in any way compensated Mrs. Erickson
or the Agency for Mrs. Erickson's consulting work.

Commissioner Erickson respectfully requests an informal written opinion as to the
above stated questions.

Very truly yours,

JOHN R. SHAW
City Attorney

JRS/ef

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Planning Commissioner James Erickson would have a conflict of interest under Government Code Section 87100 regarding voting on the Franciscan Plaza Phase II project, given that his wife performs consulting work for the Redevelopment Agency that oversees projects reviewed by the Planning Commission.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This document is a compilation of three separate 1990 letters: two incoming requests to FPPC and one agency response letter. The primary substantive matter is the May 22, 1990 request for informal opinion (Question/Facts format) regarding Commissioner Erickson's conflict of interest. The June 29 letter is a response providing additional factual information. The Harvey letter is a brief incoming request. No FPPC response/advice letter is included in this document, so conclusion sections are null.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether Planning Commissioner James Erickson would have a conflict of interest under Government Code Section 87100 regarding voting on the Franciscan Plaza Phase II project, given that his wife performs consulting work for the Redevelopment Agency that oversees projects reviewed by the Planning Commission.

embedding.summary: This is a compilation of three documents from 1990 requesting FPPC guidance: (1) Steve Harvey seeking confirmation of no conflict in voting on a planning matter involving his wife's stepfather; (2) San Juan Capistrano Redevelopment Agency providing additional information about a consulting agreement; and (3) City Attorney requesting an informal opinion on whether Planning Commissioner James Erickson has a conflict regarding voting on Franciscan Plaza Phase II, given his wife's consulting work with the Redevelopment Agency.
